component_details.name: Centralized Exchange
component_details.description: External platform for trading crypto assets.

asset_details.name: Governance Proposal
asset_details.description: Proposed changes to the Berachain protocol or dApp parameters.

asset_details.name: MEV
asset_details.description: Miner Extractable Value, earned by validators from transaction ordering.

data_flow_details.description: Protocol offers incentive tokens to a Reward Vault.

security_gap.gap_description: The integrity of the off-chain proof system for reward claiming may be compromised.

actor_risk.description: An unauthenticated external user interacting with the claiming mechanism.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A flaw in the off-chain proof generation or on-chain verification could allow unauthorized claiming of significant value.

assumptions: The backend service generating claim proofs is compromised.
The smart contract verification logic for the claim proof is flawed or insufficient.
The attacker can obtain or forge valid-looking proofs for illegitimate claims.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust cryptographic proof generation and strictly verify proofs on-chain.

description: An attacker might tamper with or forge the off-chain proof required to claim BGT or Incentive tokens from Reward Vaults, leading to unauthorized reward distribution.

data_flow_details.description: Protocol submits a governance proposal (e.g., for whitelisting).

security_gap.gap_description: The governance system may be vulnerable to disruption through high volumes of transactions.

actor_risk.description: An actor with the ability to submit transactions to the network.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Spamming transactions consumes network resources and can disrupt the governance process, although transaction costs provide some barrier.

assumptions: Transaction fees are not prohibitively high for a determined attacker.
The governance contract or network infrastructure lacks sufficient rate limiting or spam mitigation.

mitigation.strategy: Implement transaction-based rate limiting and potentially stake-and-slash requirements for proposal submission.

description: An attacker could spam the network with numerous governance proposals or voting transactions, consuming network resources and hindering legitimate governance participation.

trace_source.matched_capability: Creating your Governance Proposal, Monitor and Participate

origin.capability_match: Access and view data via BeraHub Frontend

component_details.name: Honey Swap
component_details.description: Native dApp for trading assets for $HONEY and minting $HONEY.

security_gap.gap_description: Users may be tricked into visiting a malicious replica of the BeraHub frontend.

actor_risk.description: A user attempting to access the legitimate BeraHub application.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A spoofed frontend can steal user credentials or trick users into signing malicious transactions, leading to direct asset loss.

assumptions: An attacker can create a convincing replica of the BeraHub website.
Users may not verify the website URL or security certificate.
Attacker can use phishing techniques to direct users to the fake site.

mitigation.strategy: Educate users about phishing risks, verify site authenticity, and promote official links.

description: An attacker could host a fake version of the Berachain Hub frontend to phish users' private keys or trick them into approving malicious transactions.

component_details.name: Honey Swap
component_details.description: Native dApp for trading assets for $HONEY and minting $HONEY.

asset_details.name: Incentive Token
asset_details.description: Tokens offered by protocols to validators/boosters to attract BGT emissions.

asset_details.name: Governance Proposal
asset_details.description: Proposed changes to the Berachain protocol or dApp parameters.

asset_details.name: Transaction Fee
asset_details.description: Cost paid in $BERA to execute a transaction on the network.

asset_details.name: MEV
asset_details.description: Miner Extractable Value, earned by validators from transaction ordering.

asset_details.name: LP Token
asset_details.description: Receipt token specifically for providing liquidity in BEX pools.

attack_surface.data_description: Blockchain data displayed on the frontend (balances, APYs, validator stats)

data_flow_details.description: User delegates BGT voting power to a validator.

security_gap.gap_description: Data fetched from the blockchain and displayed on the frontend may be manipulated.

actor_risk.description: A user relying on information displayed on the BeraHub frontend.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Manipulated display data can lead users to make incorrect decisions (e.g., about staking pools or swaps).

assumptions: The connection between the frontend and the RPC node is compromised.
The frontend application server is compromised, allowing data modification before display.
Users do not verify critical information via an independent block explorer.

mitigation.strategy: Fetch critical data from trusted sources and implement integrity checks. Educate users to verify important information independently.

description: An attacker could tamper with data displayed on the BeraHub frontend (e.g., token balances, pool APYs, validator stats) by compromising the data source or frontend code.

component_details.name: EVM Execution Client
component_details.description: Software responsible for executing smart contracts and managing state changes.

asset_details.description: Receipt token specifically for providing liquidity in BEX pools.

attack_surface.data_description: Signed transaction data sent to RPC endpoint

data_flow_details.description: Reward Vault sends incentive tokens to the distribution contract.

security_gap.gap_description: User wallet may connect to an untrusted blockchain node.

actor_risk.description: A user whose wallet is configured to connect to a potentially malicious RPC endpoint.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A malicious RPC can return false data, fail to broadcast transactions, or potentially attempt to compromise the user's wallet depending on RPC capabilities.

assumptions: User manually configures wallet to use an untrusted or compromised RPC endpoint.
The wallet software does not sufficiently validate RPC responses or provide clear warnings.
Attacker controls a malicious RPC endpoint that users connect to.

mitigation.strategy: Educate users on using trusted RPCs. Provide official, secure RPC endpoints. Wallet software should implement security checks and warnings.

description: A user connects their wallet to a malicious RPC endpoint, which could tamper with returned data, provide false confirmations, or fail to broadcast transactions, potentially leading to loss or manipulation of funds.
